untranslatable_reason: Signal requires a mean-reverting spread (cointegrated pair relationship) and explicit threshold parameters (lookback_period, threshold, half_life) which are marked AUTHOR_SILENT. The specific spread calculation, pair selection, and exact threshold values cannot be determined from available indicators. Z-score used as a reasonable proxy for mean reversion detection, but this is an approximation rather than the true signal logic.
translation_notes: This is a context-dependent mean reversion signal for pairs trading. Without the specific lookback_period, threshold levels, and half_life parameters, exact replication is impossible. Used zscore_20 as a general mean-reversion proxy assuming standardization of a spread around zero. Entry conditions trigger on extreme negative z-scores (potential long) and extreme positive z-scores (potential short). Exits reverse when spread returns toward mean. Backtest Sharpe of 2.1 noted but cannot be validated without parameter specifics. Suitable only for RANGING/stationary market regimes.
